macro_contract.note: Use macros in field values. Keep field names neutral because field names are not replaced at runtime.

identity.name: {{char}}
identity.core_concept: One sentence for who {{char}} is in scenes, not a trope label.

role_context.one_line: A quick usable summary of {{char}}'s current function in the story.
role_context.current_obligation: What {{char}} cannot simply walk away from.
role_context.resources_or_constraints: What {{char}} has, lacks, controls, owes, or depends on.
role_context.hidden_situation: A deeper truth that recontextualizes {{pronoun.objective}} if discovered.
role_context.stakes_if_nothing_changes: What gets worse if {{char}} keeps going as-is.
role_context.what_changes_by_act_or_phase: How {{pronoun.pos_det}} situation should evolve as the project advances.

scenario_context.project_role: What slot {{char}} fills in this specific setting or story.
scenario_context.timeline_anchor: When {{char}} enters the story, and what phase, era, or act {{pronoun.subjective}} belong{{is}} to.
location_anchors: Places strongly associated with {{char}} in this scenario.

faction_or_group_bindings.relationship: Member, outsider, enemy, dependent, informant, etc.

mechanical_hooks: Optional game/project mechanics tied to {{char}}, such as AWARENESS, DEPTH, reputation, stress, faction heat, romance route, etc.
scenario_context.relationship_to_player_at_start: How {{char}} initially knows or reads {{user}} in this scenario.
scenario_only_notes: Details that should be removed or rewritten when porting {{char}} elsewhere.

engine.core_want: What {{char}} {{is}} reaching for in life right now. Specific and present-tense.
engine.internal_need: What {{char}} actually need{{is}}, whether or not {{pronoun.subjective}} can name it.
engine.core_desire: The positive longing underneath the surface want.
engine.public_self: How {{char}} presents to people who do not know {{pronoun.objective}} well.
engine.private_self: What is actually going on under that presentation.
engine.central_lie: One false belief {{char}} uses to stay emotionally organized.
engine.emotional_wound: The old hurt or unresolved pressure that still shapes {{pronoun.objective}}.
engine.biggest_contradiction: The thing about {{char}} that does not add up on first inspection.
engine.what_they_self_deceive_about: One thing {{char}} tells {{pronoun.reflexive}} that is not quite true.

soft_boundaries: A line {{char}} resists but may bend under pressure
temptations: A choice that can pull {{char}} off-center
moral_blind_spots: Something {{char}} fails to see clearly about {{pronoun.pos_det}} own conduct
lines_never_cross: A moral or relational action {{char}} never takes
lines_might_cross: A risky action {{char}} might take if the scene earns it

physical_presence.appearance_summary: Scene-useful physical impression of {{char}}. Avoid trivia unless it changes prose.
physical_presence.movement_style: How {{char}} moves through a room, crowd, fight, conversation, or silence.
physical_presence.dress_and_objects: Clothes, tools, keepsakes, or objects that tell the truth about {{pronoun.objective}}.
sensory_details: One grounded detail the narrator can reuse sparingly

voice.speech_summary: One sentence describing how {{char}} talks in practice.
voice.vocabulary_register: Formal, casual, slangy, academic, technical, poetic, plainspoken, code-switching, or mixed. Say when it shifts.
voice.humor_style: How humor appears, if it does.
speech_tics: First small specific verbal habit {{char}} has

what_lands: A behavior, choice, or attitude that goes over well with {{char}}
what_falls_flat: A behavior, choice, or attitude that does not land with {{pronoun.objective}}
interaction_logic.gift_logic: What a thoughtful gift or meaningful gesture looks like for {{pronoun.objective}}. A principle, not a shopping list.
interaction_logic.how_they_read_people: What {{char}} notices about people and what {{pronoun.subjective}} miss{{is}}.
interaction_logic.how_they_read_user: What {{char}} notices about {{user}} first, and what takes longer.
what_builds_trust: A condition or repeated behavior that lets {{char}} trust someone
what_breaks_trust: A condition or behavior that closes {{pronoun.objective}} off

trait_expansions.trait: First trait, one or two words
trait_expansions.limit: What {{char}} will not do even with this trait active.
trait_expansions.scene_expression: How this should naturally land in narration when {{char}} {{is}} on screen.

states.default_state: How {{char}} actually behave{{is}} when nothing in particular {{is}} happening.
states.under_stress: How {{pronoun.subjective}} behave{{is}} when something is wrong.
states.when_exhausted: How {{pronoun.subjective}} behave{{is}} when tired, depleted, hungry, injured, overworked, or emotionally spent.
states.when_happy: What genuine ease looks like for {{pronoun.objective}}.
states.when_angry: How {{pronoun.subjective}} express{{is}} anger and how visible it is.
states.when_hurt: Distinct from angry. How hurt shows in {{pronoun.objective}}.
states.when_afraid: How fear changes {{pronoun.pos_det}} choices, voice, body, or attention.
states.when_trusting: What changes when {{char}} actually trusts someone.
states.when_caught_off_guard: What happens when something lands that {{pronoun.subjective}} did not expect.

relationship_texture.with_authority: How {{pronoun.subjective}} handle{{is}} authority, power, rank, or expertise.
relationship_texture.with_family_or_origin_group: How {{char}} behaves with the people or group that formed {{pronoun.objective}}.
relationship_texture.with_user_initially: What {{pronoun.pos_det}} first real conversation with {{user}} looks like.
relationship_texture.what_makes_them_open_up: The conditions that get past {{pronoun.pos_det}} guard, not the formula.

relationship_escalation.recognition: How {{char}} first starts treating {{user}} as real.
relationship_escalation.trust: What changes when trust is earned.
relationship_escalation.intimacy_or_loyalty: How closeness shows if the relationship deepens.

knows.note: How {{char}} know{{is}} them and what kind of relationship it is

doesnt_know.note: Why {{pronoun.subjective}} {{is}} not familiar with them, where this matters

arc_skeleton.notice: How {{user}} first sees {{char}} as a person beyond {{pronoun.pos_det}} surface role.
arc_skeleton.approach: How real contact with {{pronoun.objective}} becomes possible.
arc_skeleton.friction: The first meaningful test, contradiction, disagreement, or cost.
arc_skeleton.depth: What truth, vulnerability, access, or new context becomes available.
arc_skeleton.stakes: What choice or consequence now matters for {{char}}.
arc_skeleton.capstone: How {{pronoun.pos_det}} arc resolves, transforms, breaks, or remains open.

do_not_flatten_into: A flat archetype {{char}} would be misread as
do_not_overuse: A recurring beat that would get tiresome if leaned on
good_recurring_motifs: A small specific thing that can recur in scenes with {{char}}
continuity_rules: A rule the narrator should preserve across scenes
narrator_notes.retrieval_notes: When this character is in scene, retrieve voice, current pressure, states, relationship texture, and any relevant runtime state.

example_exchanges.why_it_matters: What this exchange proves about voice, values, or relationship logic.
